title: Add a Filter data by values transformation

blocks.content: The filter data by values transformation allows you to include or exclude rows based on specific values in one or more fields. It's similar to a SQL `WHERE` clause. This is useful if your data source doesn't natively filter by value, or if you want to narrow values to display when using a shared query.
To use this transformation, open an existing panel in edit mode on your dashboard.
Click **Add transformation** and select **Filter data by values**.

On **Filter type**, select whether you want an include or exclude filter. On **Condition**, select whether the filter should match **all** conditions or **any**. Use the following table as a guideline for the available conditions:

| Condition | When to use |
| --- | --- |
| Regex | Match a regex expression. |
| Is null / Is not null | Match if the value is (or isn't) null. |
| Is equal / Is not equal | Match if the value is (or isn't) equal to the specified value. |
| Contains substring / Does not contain substring | (String fields) Match on substring presence. |
| Is greater / Is greater or equal | (Numeric fields) Match values above a threshold. |
| Is lower / Is lower or equal | (Numeric fields) Match values below a threshold. |
| Is between | (Numeric fields) Match values within a range. |
Choose the field and condition for your filter. Add more conditions if needed.

steps.reftarget: div[data-testid='data-testid Transformation editor Filter data by values']
steps.description: Configure the filter field and condition in this editor, then click anywhere here when you're done.

blocks.content: Click **Save dashboard**, confirm the title, and click **Save**.

The transformation is applied automatically. If it doesn't refresh, click **Refresh** on the panel to verify the visualization looks as intended.

In the next milestone, you'll combine results from multiple queries using a join by field transformation.

---

### More to explore (optional)
